rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO. Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents. Granted in part and denied in part. Within 30 days after entry of this order, Paul Ryan Associates shall produce the summary portion of each invoice from its attorneys showing the timekeepers' names, hourly rates, and total hours per invoice, as well as the total reimbursable costs charged to Paul Ryan on each invoice. Fifteen days before trial, Paul Ryan shall produce unredacted copies of these invoices. The remaining documents sought by the motion, such as, for example, time sheets and pre-bills that were never sent to Paul Ryan by its attorneys, do not appear reasonably likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MACK FILM DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. BENEVOLENT PARTNERS, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF MACK FILM DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S Mtn For Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. The court has received a proposed order with the moving papers. Therefore no additional proposed order need be submitted by the moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JAMES KIRK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEES OF THE et al VS. MITCHELL MENAGED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT RICHARD BICARDO Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. The motion is granted; the settlement between Plaintiffs and the Settling Defendants was made in good faith within the meaning of CCP section 877.6 The declaration of Mr. Linden demonstrates that the $600,000 settlement is in the ballpark under Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. The contesting party fails to demonstrate that the motion lacks good faith and has not met its burden under CCP 877.6(d). The Cross-Complaints filed against the settling defendants are dismissed with prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FRANK L. KING VS. SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Augment Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 8, PETITIONER FRANK KING Motion To Augment Record. Petitioner's motion to augment the record is denied. As to the September 16, 2012 Social Security Administration decision, Petitioner failed to make a threshold showing that the Social Security Administration decision is relevant for the purposes of analyzing Petitioner's application for ordinary disability retirement, which is based on a different legal standard. (See Code of Civil Procedure § 1094.5(e).) As to Dr. Rosenbaum's medical report, Petitioner has failed to show that it is relevant. Dr. Rosenbaum's report was based on an exam that took place in April 2012. Petitioner has not shown how these findings are relevant to the issue whether Petitioner was incapacitated for the performance of his job duties as a patient care assistant when he retired 1 and ½ years earlier on September 1, 2010. Further, Petitioner has not shown that, even if Dr. Rosenbaum's report were relevant, that in the exercise of reasonable diligence it could not have been produced in connection with Petitioner's application for ordinary disability retirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA VS. PHILIP EDWARD KAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Robin Kay'S Motion For Set Aside/Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 24, Defendant Robin Kay's Motion For Set Aside/Vacate Judgment. Denied. The hearing will be at 9:00am, not 9:30am. Neither Rule 9.23 nor constitutional due process require that there be notice to a defendant prior to the court's exercise of its mandatory duty to enter judgment upon the request of the State Bar when the State bar submits the required paperwork. The State Bar's failure to serve Ms. Kay or Mr. Kay's counsel with the judgment after it was entered does not render the judgment void or otherwise require or even permit that the judgment be set aside. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the State Bar is required to prepare a proposed order with their name and address in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing if the tentative ruling is not contested. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID PIVTORAK VS. DAVID CARL ANDERSON, DBA LAW OFFICE OF DAVID C. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT WADE & LOWE, P.C. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Hearing required. Judge Miller will disclose at the hearing that she presided at mandatory settlement conferences in the underlying case (CGC-10-500849) on June 3, 20, and 22, 2011 (according to the Registry of Actions). rulings.Case Title: ROGER M. BOONE VS. JOSEPH H. CABALLERO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT JOSEPH CABALLERO Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Denied in its entirety as to motion to compel and motion to stay proceedings pending arbitration. Defendant has not met his burden to show the existence of the arbitration agreement and that the dispute is covered by the agreement. The promissory note does not contain an agreement to arbitrate. There is no evidence the parties ever orally agreed to arbitrate disputes regarding the promissory note. The arbitration agreement in the Investment Management Agreements do not apply to the promissory note. The Investment Management Agreements are signed by two different trusts, the Roger M. Boone Trust and Roger M. Boone 2001 Charitable Remainder Unitrust. Plaintiff is not a party to those agreements in his individual capacity. The arbitration clauses in the Investment Management Agreements are limited to the subjects of those two agreements. The client investment agreements do not contemplate the loan agreements. Although Defendant brought an arbitration proceeding against Perigon Partners LLC, Perigon Wealth Management, LLC, and other Perigon officers because of Plaintiff's collections efforts, this does not make Plaintiff a party to arbitration by agreement or as a third party that would be subject to arbitration, nor does it provide a basis to stay these proceedings pending that arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRYAN ZIMMERMAN et al VS. WONDERBAR LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion For A Protective Order Re Pltf'S Propounded Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT WONDERBAR LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY, JAY SIEGAN Motion For A Protective Order Re Pltf'S Propounded Discovery And Request For Sanctions. (PART 1 OF 2) Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. (CONTINUED, SEE PART 2 OF 2) = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRYAN ZIMMERMAN et al VS. WONDERBAR LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Wonderbar Llc And Jay Siegan'S Motion For A Protective Order Re Plaintiff'S Propounded Discovery And Request For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 16, 2013, Line 4, Wonderbar LLC And Jay Siegan'S Motion For A Protective Order Re Plaintiff's Propounded Discovery And Request For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2). (CONTINUED FROM PART 1 OF 2) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. A number of the discovery requests are duplicative. Defendants Wonderbar LLC and Jay Siegan shall each separately respond to Plaintiff Bennet's Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One, modified as follows: The last sentence of Definition 4(d) "EMPLOYEE" is modified to read, "For purposes of these interrogatories, EMPLOYEE refers to Bennet, Zimmerman, Schiaretti, Leeb, Justin, Yee, and Sosa. Both defendants shall respond separately to Plaintiff Bennet's Form Interrogatories - General Set One, but need not respond to nos. 4.1, 4.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 and 50.6 since these interrogatories request the same information as previously requested in the Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One. Defendants need not answer the Form Interrogatories - Employment Law and Form Interrogatories - General propounded by any other plaintiff. Both defendants shall respond to each Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions. Defendants shall each separately respond to every interrogatory in Plaintiff Bennet's Special Interrogatories - Set One. Defendants shall also each separately respond to plaintiffs Zimmerman, Schiaretti, Leeb, Justin, Yee, and Sosa's Special Interrogatories 1 - 26, but need not respond to Special Interrogatories 27 - 35. Defendants shall each respond separately to Plaintiff Bennett's Requests for Production - Set One in its entirety. Defendants shall also respond separately to Plaintiffs Zimmerman, Schiaretti, Leeb, Justin, Yee, and Sosa's Requests for Production Nos. 1, 3 - 16, 20, 21, 25 - 28, 31 - 36, 38, 39, 44, 45, and 46, but need not respond to their other requests for production. The instruction in this order that Defendants shall respond to any given discovery request is not to be taken as a ruling that Defendants have waived any objections or claims of privilege with respect to the discovery request. Each party's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
